в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
This tumor harbors alterations in KRAS, TP53, SMAD4, and CDKN2A вЂ” the four most commonly co-altered genes in pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, consistent with the submitted diagnosis. Several additional alterations of uncertain or investigational significance are identified. Of particular clinical relevance: an ATM frameshift variant (p.V2716fs) is identified at a VAF of 6%, raising the question of a germline pathogenic variant in ATM; germline confirmation testing of a blood specimen is strongly recommended. A low-VAF BRCA2 splice site variant is also identified and warrants germline assessment, though the low VAF suggests a subclonal somatic event or technical artifact. Tumor mutational burden is low and microsatellite status is stable (MSS), indicating that immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy is unlikely to yield benefit in this tumor. No targetable alterations qualifying for approved therapies (e.g., NTRK fusion, FGFR alteration, HER2 amplification, MSI-high, BRCA1/2 pathogenic germline mutation) are identified at this time.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

1. KRAS вЂ” Missense Mutation
   Variant:             p.G12V (c.35G>T); exon 2, codon 12
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 42%
   Classification:      Pathogenic вЂ” Oncogenic Driver
   Functional significance: Substitution of glycine for valine at codon 12 of KRAS results in constitutive GTP-bound activation of the RAS/MAPK and PI3K/AKT/mTOR signaling cascades, rendering the RAS protein resistant to GAP-mediated GTP hydrolysis. KRAS G12V is the second most common activating KRAS point mutation in pancreatic cancer (after G12D) and is present in approximately 10-15% of KRAS-mutant PDAC cases.
   Prevalence in PDAC:  KRAS mutations occur in >90% of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinomas; G12V represents approximately 25% of these.
   Clinical context:    KRAS G12V is a well-established oncogenic driver in PDAC. There are no approved direct KRAS G12V inhibitors as of this report for pancreatic adenocarcinoma, though investigational agents targeting KRAS G12V (e.g., MRTX1133, adagrasib analogs) are under active clinical investigation in PDAC. The VAF of 42% is consistent with a clonal event in the presence of approximately 40% tumor cellularity. This variant is NOT associated with any germline cancer predisposition syndrome.
   Therapy Implication: Investigational only; no FDA-approved targeted therapy available for KRAS G12V in pancreatic cancer.

2. TP53 вЂ” Missense Mutation
   Variant:             p.R248W (c.742C>T); exon 7, DNA-binding domain
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 38%
   Classification:      Pathogenic вЂ” Tumor Suppressor Gene, Loss-of-Function (dominant negative)
   Functional significance: Arginine 248 is a DNA-contact residue in the p53 DNA-binding domain; the R248W substitution is among the most common and functionally characterized TP53 hotspot mutations. This alteration confers loss of wild-type p53 transcriptional activity and, importantly, a dominant negative effect that can suppress the activity of any residual wild-type p53 from the second allele. R248W also confers gain-of-function (GOF) oncogenic properties including enhanced cell migration, drug resistance, and altered metabolic reprogramming. Loss of heterozygosity at the TP53 locus is common in PDAC and is consistent with the high VAF observed relative to tumor cellularity.
   Prevalence in PDAC:  TP53 mutations occur in 50-75% of PDAC; R248W is one of the most recurrent hotspot variants.
   Therapy Implication: No approved targeted therapies directly exploit TP53 R248W in pancreatic cancer. Investigational APR-246 (eprenetapopt) and related compounds restoring p53 function are not specifically active against GOF R248W in this context. TP53 mutation status may inform prognosis (generally adverse in PDAC).

3. SMAD4 вЂ” Homozygous Deletion
   Variant:             Copy number = 0 (homozygous deletion)
   Chromosomal locus:   18q21.2
   Classification:      Pathogenic вЂ” Tumor Suppressor Gene, Biallelic Inactivation
   Functional significance: SMAD4 (DPC4 вЂ” Deleted in Pancreatic Cancer locus 4) encodes the central mediator of canonical TGF-ОІ/BMP signaling. Homozygous deletion of SMAD4 results in complete loss of SMAD4 protein, abrogating TGF-ОІ-mediated tumor suppression. In pancreatic cancer, SMAD4 loss is associated with widespread metastatic dissemination and a more aggressive clinical phenotype. This molecular finding is concordant with the complete loss of SMAD4 protein expression by immunohistochemistry reported on the prior diagnostic biopsy (DPC4 IHC negative).
   Prevalence in PDAC:  SMAD4 is deleted or mutated in approximately 55% of PDAC; homozygous deletion accounts for approximately 30% of inactivating events.
   Therapy Implication: No approved targeted therapies. SMAD4 loss may have prognostic implications, and some evidence suggests SMAD4-null PDAC may be less likely to achieve durable locoregional control. SMAD4 status has been evaluated as a predictor of benefit from local therapy vs. systemic-only approaches (SMAD4 loss associated with higher systemic relapse risk).

4. CDKN2A вЂ” Homozygous Deletion
   Variant:             Copy number = 0 (homozygous deletion); affects CDKN2A (p16/INK4A) and CDKN2B (p15/INK4B) loci
   Chromosomal locus:   9p21.3
   Classification:      Pathogenic вЂ” Tumor Suppressor Gene, Biallelic Inactivation
   Functional significance: CDKN2A encodes the cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor p16/INK4A, a critical negative regulator of the CDK4/6-RB axis. Homozygous deletion of CDKN2A results in unrestrained CDK4/6 activity, leading to constitutive RB phosphorylation and release of E2F transcription factors driving cell cycle progression from G1 to S phase. Concomitant deletion of CDKN2B further impairs TGF-ОІ-dependent growth arrest. This alteration is among the earliest and most prevalent events in pancreatic carcinogenesis, often arising in the precursor PanIN lesion stage.
   Prevalence in PDAC:  CDKN2A inactivation occurs in >90% of PDAC, predominantly via homozygous deletion or promoter hypermethylation.
   Therapy Implication: CDK4/6 inhibitors (e.g., palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib) have been investigated in PDAC with CDKN2A loss, but clinical benefit has been modest in unselected PDAC. CDKN2A loss may render the tumor theoretically more sensitive to CDK4/6-RB axis targeting in the context of clinical trials.

5. ATM вЂ” Frameshift Mutation  *** GERMLINE TESTING RECOMMENDED ***
   Variant:             p.V2716fs (c.8145del, single nucleotide deletion); exon 58, FAT/FATC domain
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 6%
   Classification:      Pathogenic (frameshift, predicted loss of function); Germline vs. Somatic: UNCERTAIN вЂ” germline testing of peripheral blood recommended
   Functional significance: ATM (Ataxia-Telangiectasia Mutated) encodes a serine/threonine kinase central to the DNA damage response, particularly double-strand break (DSB) repair via homologous recombination (HR) and non-homologous end joining (NHEJ). Frameshift mutations in ATM are predicted to produce a truncated, non-functional protein. Loss of ATM function impairs DSB repair, potentially conferring sensitivity to DNA-damaging agents (platinum compounds, PARP inhibitors). The VAF of 6% is low relative to the estimated tumor cellularity (~40%), which could reflect: (a) a germline heterozygous variant with loss of heterozygosity (LOH) not occurring in this tumor (expected VAF ~50% if clonal-germline without LOH, or lower if LOH in only a subpopulation); (b) a subclonal somatic mutation; or (c) technical factors. Given the low VAF and the pathogenicity of this frameshift, germline ATM testing is strongly recommended. Germline pathogenic ATM variants are associated with a 2- to 3-fold increased risk of pancreatic adenocarcinoma, and with increased risks of breast, prostate, and other cancers. ATM-deficient tumors may have enhanced sensitivity to PARP inhibitors and platinum-based chemotherapy, which has therapeutic implications particularly in the advanced/metastatic setting.
   Clinical note:        If confirmed germline, family members (first-degree relatives) should be offered genetic counseling and cascade testing. Per NCCPA/ASCO guidelines, germline ATM pathogenic variants may qualify patients for enhanced pancreatic cancer surveillance programs in unaffected relatives.
   GERMLINE TESTING STRONGLY RECOMMENDED for this variant.

6. BRCA2 вЂ” Splice Site Variant  *** GERMLINE TESTING RECOMMENDED ***
   Variant:             c.8331+1G>A (canonical splice donor site, intron 18)
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 5%
   Classification:      Likely Pathogenic вЂ” splice site variant affecting a canonical GT splice donor dinucleotide; Germline vs. Somatic: UNCERTAIN, likely subclonal somatic or technical artifact given low VAF вЂ” germline testing recommended
   Functional significance: The c.8331+1G>A variant disrupts the invariant +1 splice donor nucleotide of intron 18 of BRCA2, predicted to cause aberrant splicing with likely exon 18 retention or exon skipping, leading to a frameshift and premature stop codon. BRCA2 is a key mediator of homologous recombination repair; biallelic BRCA2 loss creates a state of BRCAness with increased sensitivity to platinum compounds and PARP inhibitors. In PDAC, germline BRCA2 pathogenic variants occur in approximately 4-7% of cases, particularly in Ashkenazi Jewish and other high-risk populations, and confer substantially elevated lifetime risk of pancreatic cancer. The very low VAF (5%) is below the level expected for a clonal somatic or germline event at the estimated tumor cellularity; this may represent a subclonal somatic event arising late in tumor evolution, an artifact of sequencing in the FFPE specimen, or a true low-level somatic event. Germline testing should be performed to exclude a germline origin.
   Note:                 If this variant is confirmed to be somatic only and subclonal, its clinical significance for therapeutic selection is limited. If confirmed germline, BRCA2 status would qualify the patient for olaparib maintenance therapy if metastatic disease were to develop in the future (per FDA labeling for BRCA-mutant pancreatic cancer), and for BRCA2-related cancer surveillance and family counseling.
   GERMLINE TESTING RECOMMENDED to clarify origin of this variant.

7. ARID1A вЂ” Nonsense Mutation
   Variant:             p.R1276* (c.3826C>T); exon 20
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 12%
   Classification:      Likely Pathogenic вЂ” Tumor Suppressor, Loss of Function
   Functional significance: ARID1A encodes a component of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex (specifically BAF complex, AT-rich interactive domain 1A). Nonsense mutations such as p.R1276* are predicted to result in a truncated, non-functional protein, impairing chromatin remodeling and transcriptional regulation. ARID1A mutations occur in approximately 6-10% of PDAC and are associated with impaired DNA mismatch repair pathway interactions in some tumor types; however, this tumor is MSS, suggesting that ARID1A loss alone is insufficient to drive microsatellite instability in this context. There are currently no approved targeted therapies specifically directed against ARID1A-mutant pancreatic cancer. Preclinical data suggest that ARID1A-deficient tumors may have synthetic lethality with ARID1B inhibition or EZH2 inhibition, but these remain investigational. The subclonal VAF of 12% (below the expected clonal ~42% based on KRAS) suggests this mutation may have arisen as a secondary, clonal evolution event.

8. RNF43 вЂ” Frameshift Mutation
   Variant:             p.G659fs (c.1975delG); exon 9
   Variant Allele Fraction (VAF): 8%
   Classification:      Likely Pathogenic вЂ” Potential Loss of Function
   Functional significance: RNF43 encodes a transmembrane E3 ubiquitin ligase that functions as a negative regulator of the Wnt/Frizzled signaling pathway by ubiquitinating Frizzled receptors for lysosomal degradation. Loss-of-function mutations in RNF43 lead to constitutive activation of the Wnt/ОІ-catenin pathway. RNF43 frameshift mutations occur in approximately 5-10% of PDAC and are more commonly associated with pancreatic mucinous neoplasms/intraductal papillary mucinous neoplasms (IPMN)-associated adenocarcinoma, though they may occur in conventional PDAC. RNF43-mutant tumors may theoretically be sensitive to porcupine inhibitors (e.g., LGK-974, WNT-974) that block Wnt ligand secretion, but no such agents are currently approved. This low-VAF subclonal alteration may represent intratumoral heterogeneity or a late evolutionary event.

   Gene/Locus        Alteration            Copy Number     Significance
   в”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђв”Ђ
   SMAD4 (18q21.2)   Homozygous deletion   0               Pathogenic (see Section 1)
   CDKN2A (9p21.3)   Homozygous deletion   0               Pathogenic (see Section 1)
   CDKN2B (9p21.3)   Homozygous deletion   0               Co-deleted with CDKN2A
   MYC (8q24.21)     Low-level gain        ~4-5 copies     Variant of Uncertain Significance

Note on MYC gain: Low-level amplification/gain of MYC (8q24.21, encoding c-Myc) is identified. MYC gain occurs in approximately 10-20% of PDAC and is associated with enhanced glycolytic metabolism, elevated proliferation, and potentially with resistance to multiple cytotoxic agents. There are no approved anti-MYC targeted therapies; investigational approaches include BET bromodomain inhibitors and direct MYC inhibitors in early clinical development. This finding is considered investigational.

SECTION 7: OVERALL MOLECULAR PROFILE SUMMARY AND CLINICAL INTERPRETATION
в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ

This tumor demonstrates the classic four-gene alteration signature of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma: concurrent KRAS activating mutation (G12V), TP53 missense mutation (R248W), SMAD4 homozygous deletion, and CDKN2A homozygous deletion. This co-mutational signature is present in approximately 10-15% of PDAC cases and generally represents a fully evolved, transcriptionally dysregulated adenocarcinoma with poor prognosis in the absence of surgical resection. The presence of SMAD4 homozygous deletion in this borderline resectable context is noteworthy: SMAD4-null PDAC has been associated with hematogenous and peritoneal dissemination patterns in the neoadjuvant setting, and has been proposed as a biomarker to identify patients who may benefit more from prolonged systemic therapy prior to surgical reassessment. Concomitant CDKN2A homozygous deletion adds additional cell cycle dysregulation; together with SMAD4 loss, this profile represents a highly aggressive molecular phenotype.

There is no currently approved molecularly targeted therapy for this patient's tumor based on the detected somatic alterations. The tumor is MSS/TMB-Low/PD-L1 CPS 2, precluding immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy based on current FDA approvals. No NTRK, FGFR, or HER2 alterations are present.

The most actionable clinical implication of this report is the ATM frameshift variant (p.V2716fs, VAF 6%), which warrants urgent germline testing. Germline ATM pathogenic variants are associated with a modestly elevated risk of PDAC (approximately 2-3x population risk) and, more importantly in the therapeutic context, predict for enhanced sensitivity to DNA damage repair pathway-targeted agents including PARP inhibitors and platinum-based chemotherapy. While this is most immediately relevant in the metastatic disease setting, the identification of a germline ATM variant has implications for: (a) current treatment selection (platinum-containing chemotherapy regimens such as FOLFIRINOX may be particularly advantageous in ATM-deficient tumors); (b) potential eligibility for clinical trials evaluating PARP inhibitors in germline ATM-mutant PDAC; and (c) genetic counseling and cascade testing of first-degree family members at increased cancer risk.

The low-VAF BRCA2 splice site variant (c.8331+1G>A, VAF 5%) is of uncertain significance in this tumor-only assay. If confirmed germline, this would identify the patient as a BRCA2 carrier with therapeutic implications (PARP inhibitor eligibility, clinical trial access). Germline testing is recommended. If confirmed somatic-only and subclonal, clinical impact on current treatment is limited.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Germline genetic testing of a peripheral blood or saliva specimen is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED to evaluate for germline status of the ATM p.V2716fs frameshift variant. Concurrent evaluation of the BRCA2 c.8331+1G>A splice site variant should be included. Given the clinical diagnosis of pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma in the absence of a positive family history, and in the context of these potentially germline variants, referral to certified genetic counseling is advised prior to germline testing to facilitate pre- and post-test counseling. Per NCCN Guidelines for Genetic/Familial High-Risk Assessment: Breast, Ovarian, Pancreatic, and Prostate (Version 2.2024), germline testing is recommended for all patients with pancreatic adenocarcinoma regardless of family history.

в”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓв”Ѓ
Variant pathogenicity classification based on ClinVar, COSMIC, OncoKB (Memorial Sloan Kettering), CIViC database, and published literature. Copy number analysis performed using normalized read-depth ratio compared to a matched process-normal reference. Fusion detection by RNA-based hybrid capture and sequencing of selected intronic regions. TMB calculated from somatic non-synonymous coding mutations per megabase of sequenced coding genome, after filtering germline polymorphisms. MSI assessed by both fragment analysis (capillary electrophoresis) and NGS-based MSIsensor score. PD-L1 scored by certified pathologist using 22C3 pharmDx assay (Dako/Agilent) on the FFPE biopsy specimen.

This report reflects the genomic profile of the analyzed specimen and is limited by specimen quality, tumor heterogeneity, and the scope of the gene panel (324 genes; whole-genome alterations outside the panel are not detected). Results should be interpreted in the context of full clinical, pathological, and radiographic data by a qualified physician. This assay is for research and clinical decision support; it does not constitute a standalone treatment recommendation.
